titleStmt.title: President Roosevelt to the Shahanshah of Iran ( (Mohammad Reza Shah Pahlavi))

div.head: President Roosevelt to the Shahanshah of Iran ( Mohammad Reza Shah Pahlavi )Transmitted to the Chargé in Iran by the Secretary of State in instruction 483, September 6, for delivery to the Shah.
div.p: My Dear Shah Mohammad Reza: I was pleased and touched to receive, on my return to Washington, your friendly communication of June 17, 1944.

It is unfortunate that I was prevented by pressing matters, including my extended visit to the Pacific Coast and Hawaii, from receiving your communication directly from the hands of Mr. Ebtehadj. I
should have been glad to learn from him at first-hand of your health and of developments in Your Majesty’s intention to endeavor to intensify the cooperation already existing between Iran and the United States, both now and after the war.

Although the pressures under which I have labored during the past year have not always made it possible for me to demonstrate, in ways I should have liked, my special interest in and personal regard for Iran, I know I can rely on Your Majesty’s deep understanding of the exigencies which have demanded my attention during these past months. Iran and America have every reason to be close friends. Patience and understanding on both our parts are needed. If matters of irritation arise, I hope you will let me know your views in the spirit of frankness and cordiality which I hope will always characterize our relations. General Hurley has spoken to me often of Your Majesty and has assured me that you would understand and reciprocate my sentiments.
With the warmest personal regards and wishes for Your Majesty’s health and the welfare of your people, I am
